chemistry_overall: All candidates show suboptimal conversion (0.51-0.76 vs target 0.85) due to insufficient residence time. Candidates 13 and 14 exceed bench length limits. Candidate 15 exceeds bench length limit and must be blocked. Candidates 4 and 5 show acceptable performance with good mixing, while candidate 3 has dual mixing failures. Candidates 16 and 17 exceed bench length limits and must be blocked. Candidate 18 is viable but needs concentration adjustment for better conversion. All candidates show acceptable performance with no hard gates. Candidate 7 achieves best conversion (86%) but has long coil length. All candidates show suboptimal conversion (<0.85) with candidate 10 performing best at 86% conversion despite long tubing length. Both candidates show suboptimal conversion due to insufficient residence time, but candidate 22 exceeds bench length limits.

kinetics_overall: All candidates show undersized residence times with conversions 0.51-0.76, well below target 0.85. Candidates 13-14 exceed bench length limits. All candidates show undersized residence times for optimal kinetics (tau_kinetics=60 min target). Candidate 3 has dual hard-gate failures making it unacceptable. All candidates show poor kinetics performance with conversions well below target. Hard-gated candidates 16, 17, and 6 face length or mixing limitations. All candidates show reasonable kinetics with conversions 0.51-0.86, though most fall short of 0.85 target. Candidate 7 achieves best conversion but has long coil length. All candidates show undersized residence times for target conversion (X<0.85), with candidate 10 performing best at 86% conversion. Both candidates show undersized residence times for optimal kinetics with conversions below 0.85 target. Candidate 22 exceeds bench length limits.

fluidics_overall: All candidates show suboptimal conversion (0.51-0.76 vs target 0.85) due to insufficient residence time. Candidates 13-14 exceed bench length limits. Candidates 4 and 5 show acceptable performance with good mixing and reasonable conversions. Candidates 15 and 3 have significant issues requiring blocking or major revision. All candidates show suboptimal conversion rates and mixing issues. Candidates 16 and 17 exceed bench length limits. All candidates show acceptable fluidic performance with low pressure drops and reasonable Reynolds numbers. Candidate 7 achieves best conversion despite long coil length. All candidates show acceptable fluidics with low pressure drops and adequate Reynolds numbers. Conversion efficiency varies significantly across designs. Both candidates show suboptimal conversion but are fluidically viable. Candidate 22 exceeds bench length limits.

safety_overall: All candidates show low pressure drops and safe operating conditions, but suffer from suboptimal conversion rates and excessive tubing lengths. Multiple candidates have hard gate failures or significant performance issues. Only candidates 4 and 5 are viable with acceptable safety profiles. Candidates 16 and 17 exceed bench length limits and should be blocked. Candidates 18 and 6 have performance issues but are technically feasible. All candidates show safe pressure levels well below pump capacity. Conversions range from 51-86% with residence time optimization needed for some candidates. All candidates operate safely at 120°C with low pressure drops and adequate BPR settings. Conversion efficiency varies, with candidate 10 achieving best performance. Both candidates show low conversion rates and candidate 22 exceeds bench length limits. Candidate 12 is safer with shorter tubing and lower pressure drop.
